title: Hungary taps Northrop Grumman for first national geostationary communications satellite
summary: Hungary has selected Northrop Grumman to build its first national geostationary communications satellite. Additionally, 4iG has partnered with L3Harris and Apex for defense systems and satellite manufacturing.

title: Starfish Space raises more than $100 million
summary: Starfish Space has secured over $100 million in funding to expand the production of its satellite servicing spacecraft. This investment will help the company scale its operations in the growing in-space servicing sector.

title: NASA’s new moon base project requires operational technology systems in space, but they are vulnerable.
summary: NASA announced a $20 billion plan to build a moon base instead of a lunar orbit space station, with newly appointed Administrator Jared Isaacman revealing the shift on March 24, 2026. The project requires operational space technology systems, which are currently vulnerable.

title: Nominate space industry leaders for the 2026 SpaceNews Icon Awards
summary: SpaceNews is calling for nominations for its 2026 SpaceNews Icon Awards to recognize influential leaders in the space industry. The awards aim to honor innovators, collaborators, and change-makers who have made significant contributions to the sector.

title: Earth observation operators push to deliver satellite images within minutes
summary: Earth observation companies are striving to deliver high-resolution satellite images within minutes of capture, as demonstrated by Vantor's real-time showcase. A 30-centimeter-resolution image was processed and delivered in just 13 minutes during a sales event.

title: Artemis 2 swings around the moon
summary: The Artemis 2 mission successfully completed a lunar flyby with four astronauts from the U.S. and Canada, marking the farthest human travel from Earth to date. This milestone brings NASA's Artemis program closer to returning humans to the moon.

title: Moog Highlights Growing Satellite Bus Capabilities with Full‑Scale METEOR Reveal at Space Symposium
summary: Moog Inc. showcased its METEOR satellite bus capabilities at the Space Symposium, highlighting advancements in satellite bus technology. The reveal demonstrates Moog's growing role in the satellite industry.

title: FOSSA targets Japan’s defense market as larger smallsats expand capabilities
summary: Spanish startup FOSSA Systems is entering Japan's defense market by partnering with a local firm to expand its small satellite capabilities beyond picosatellites. The move reflects a broader trend of using more capable smallsats for government applications.

title: The moon base has a hardware plan. It needs a software strategy, too.
summary: A former SpaceX employee highlights the need for a software strategy to complement the hardware plans for a moon base. The article emphasizes the importance of documentation and training in space operations, drawing from their experience at SpaceX.

title: Seagate Space Signs MOU with Firefly Aerospace to Collaborate on Offshore Launch Infrastructure for Alpha
summary: Seagate Space Corporation and Firefly Aerospace have signed an MOU to collaborate on developing offshore launch infrastructure for the Alpha rocket. The partnership aims to enhance launch capabilities and support space missions.

title: The Artemis 2 space toilet is actually working fine. But there is another problem
summary: The space toilet for Artemis 2 is functioning properly, but engineers are addressing an issue with emptying the tank.

title: 'Project Hail Mary' sound designers used surprising animal sounds to create Rocky's musical alien voice (interview)
summary: The sound designers for 'Project Hail Mary' used unexpected animal sounds to craft the musical voice of the alien character Rocky. They aimed to convey Rocky's unique 'sonic soul' through these unconventional audio choices.

title: Relive Artemis 2's epic moon flyby with these amazing photos
summary: NASA's Artemis 2 astronauts shared detailed images from their historic moon flyby mission. The crew documented their journey around the moon with stunning photographs.

title: A satellite just captured images of a tsunami being born. Here's why that's a big deal
summary: A satellite captured images of a tsunami forming off Kamchatka in 2025, providing unprecedented insights into how tsunamis are generated. The observations were made using the SWOT satellite, marking a significant advancement in tsunami research.
company: SWOT (Surface Water and Ocean Topography mission)

title: Artemis 2 captures historic 'Earthset' photo | Space photo of the day for April 7, 2026
summary: Artemis 2 astronauts captured a historic 'Earthset' photo during their lunar flyby on Flight Day 6. This marks a significant moment in the Artemis 2 mission.

title: Watch Comet MAPS get destroyed by cataclysmic fragmentation near the sun
summary: Comet MAPS fragmented catastrophically near the sun, ending hopes of a bright display in the evening sky this week. The comet's destruction highlights the unpredictable nature of cometary behavior.

title: 'Unreal' solar eclipse: Artemis 2 crew just saw one of the rarest sights in spaceflight history
summary: The Artemis 2 crew witnessed a rare solar eclipse from beyond the moon during their lunar flyby, marking a historic moment in spaceflight. This event is considered one of the rarest sights in spaceflight history.

title: The Artemis 2 astronauts just flew by the moon. Lunar scientists can't wait for what's next
summary: The Artemis 2 astronauts completed a flyby of the moon, marking a significant milestone in NASA's lunar program. Lunar scientists express excitement about the future of human moon missions with modern advancements.

title: Moon milestones: A rundown of Artemis 2's many spaceflight firsts
summary: NASA's Artemis 2 mission marked the first crewed lunar flyby in over 50 years and achieved multiple spaceflight milestones. The mission introduced several groundbreaking firsts beyond the crewed lunar flyby.

title: NASA’s Artemis II Crew Beams Official Moon Flyby Photos to Earth
summary: NASA's Artemis II astronauts captured the first flyby images of the Moon's far side, revealing unseen regions and a rare in-space solar eclipse during their historic test flight.

title: Earthset
summary: The Artemis II crew captured a view of Earth setting from the far side of the Moon on April 6, 2026, documenting lunar terrain features such as impact craters and ancient lava flows.

title: Experience the Rollout of SLS Hardware for Artemis III
summary: NASA invites digital creators and social media users to witness the rollout of the SLS core stage for Artemis III from Michoud Assembly Facility to Kennedy Space Center.

title: NASA to Host Media Call with Artemis II Crew on Way Home from Moon
summary: NASA will host a media call with the Artemis II crew on April 8 at 9:45 p.m. EDT to discuss their historic mission around the Moon. The virtual news conference will feature astronauts Reid Wiseman, Victor Glover, Christina Koch, and a CSA representative.

title: NASA’s Artemis II Crew Eclipses Record for Farthest Human Spaceflight
summary: NASA's Artemis II mission crew set a new record for the farthest human spaceflight, with updates to Orion's closest lunar approach and crew achievements. The mission marks a significant milestone in human space exploration.

title: NASA’s Northrop Grumman CRS-24 Mission Overview
summary: NASA's Northrop Grumman CRS-24 mission will deliver 11,000 pounds of science and supplies to the International Space Station using the Cygnus XL spacecraft. This marks the second flight of the upgraded, more cargo-capable Cygnus XL variant.

title: Night Sky Network Celebrates Artemis II
summary: The Night Sky Network celebrated the Artemis II mission's milestone on April 6, 2026, when the crew traveled farther than any humans in history by orbiting the far side of the Moon. The event can be rewatched on NASA's YouTube channel or NASA+.

title: Artemis II breaks record, conducts lunar flyby
summary: Artemis II achieved a record lunar flyby with the Orion spacecraft Integrity, successfully carrying astronauts Reid Wiseman, Victor Glover, Christina Koch, and Jeremy Hansen. This mission marks a significant milestone in NASA's Artemis program.

title: Launch Preview: Falcon 9 to loft Cygnus to ISS, Minotaur IV to launch from California
summary: A Falcon 9 rocket is set to launch a Cygnus spacecraft to the International Space Station (ISS), while a Minotaur IV rocket is scheduled to launch from California. The news comes as NASA's Artemis II mission nears its halfway point.

title: Orion Spacecraft Races Toward Historic Lunar Flyby in Artemis II Mission
summary: NASA's Artemis II mission is in its mid-flight phase as the Orion spacecraft approaches a historic lunar flyby. This marks a significant milestone for the mission carrying the first crewed lunar flyby since Apollo.

title: Artemis II Orion performs TLI Burn — Now headed to the Moon
summary: NASA's Orion spacecraft successfully performed the Trans-Lunar Injection (TLI) burn, marking a historic milestone as it heads to the Moon for the Artemis II mission, the first crewed lunar mission in over 50 years.

title: Artemis II returns humanity to the Moon with launch on Wednesday evening
summary: The news discusses the Artemis II mission, which aims to return humanity to the Moon with a launch scheduled for Wednesday evening. The post is from NASASpaceFlight.com.

title: Launch Preview: SLS, Falcon 9, Atlas V, and Soyuz launches comprise busy launch manifest
summary: Nine launches are scheduled this week, including SLS, Falcon 9, Atlas V, and Soyuz rockets, with liftoffs planned from Florida, California, and Russia.

title: America’s Rocket Factory comes Full Circle with Artemis
summary: NASA's Artemis program marks a significant milestone as America's Rocket Factory completes preparations for crewed missions beyond low Earth orbit. The initiative represents a full-circle moment in space exploration, reviving human lunar ambitions.

title: SpaceX delivers 119 payloads to Sun-synchronous orbit on Transporter 16
summary: SpaceX successfully launched 119 payloads to Sun-synchronous orbit from California aboard the Transporter 16 mission. This marks another milestone in SpaceX's rideshare program, expanding access to space for multiple customers.

title: NASA outlines Moon Base plans, pivots on Gateway
summary: NASA announced detailed plans for a Moon base during its 'Ignition' event, shifting focus away from the Lunar Gateway. This marks a significant pivot in the agency's lunar exploration strategy.

title: Isar Aerospace scrubs second launch of Spectrum rocket
summary: Isar Aerospace, a European private space launch provider based in Germany, has scrubbed the second launch attempt of its Spectrum rocket.

title: ESA Delivers: 50 years booklet
summary: ESA celebrates its 50th anniversary by releasing a booklet highlighting 50 major achievements over the past five decades. The publication underscores the agency's contributions to space exploration and technology.

title: Graphene and lasers for space propulsion
summary: An international research team tested ultralight graphene aerogels with lasers during ESA's parabolic flight campaign in May 2025, observing significant propulsion in microgravity conditions. The study, led by Université Libre de Bruxelles and Khalifa University, demonstrated that light propulsion could be controlled by adjusting the laser, with potential applications for solar sails and satellite positioning.

title: A silicon chip that can do it all
summary: A new silicon chip has been developed with versatile capabilities, potentially revolutionizing multiple applications in the space industry. The innovation could impact various sectors, including satellite technology and onboard computing.

title: Proba-3’s Coronagraph is alive!
summary: The Proba-3 mission's Coronagraph spacecraft has re-established contact with ground control after a month-long communication loss due to an anomaly. The mission team confirmed the spacecraft is operational and has 'phoned home.'

title: Upcoming ESA–Industry events on CM25 implementation
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) is hosting a series of events to present the follow-up to the Council Meeting at Ministerial Level 2025 (CM25), focusing on planned activities, priorities, and industrial opportunities. These events aim to facilitate direct exchange between ESA and the industrial community.

title: Plato readies for space-like tests
summary: ESA's Plato exoplanet-hunting mission is undergoing space-like tests in the Large Space Simulator, with 26 ultra-sensitive cameras monitoring 150,000 stars. The spacecraft is scheduled for launch on an Ariane 6 rocket in January 2027.

title: Former NASA astronaut Jeanette Epps visits ESTEC
summary: Former NASA astronaut Jeanette Epps visited ESTEC, the European Space Agency's technical heart. This visit highlights international collaboration in space exploration.

title: ESA rolls out new costing software for its suppliers
summary: ESA will release ECOS version 6 on 30 March 2026, requiring all suppliers to use the new costing software for financial proposals in ESA tender actions.

title: The tiny lens antenna with big potential
summary: A PhD researcher developed a 3D-printed, football-sized lens antenna to enhance satellite navigation resilience against interference while being cost-effective. The compact design addresses the limitations of bulky and expensive traditional antennas.

title: Advocates Ready For NASA Science Funding Fight, Part II
summary: The Trump administration proposed an $18.8 billion NASA budget with significant cuts to science and STEM missions. Advocates and lawmakers are pushing back against these reductions.

title: Starfish Closes $100M+ Series B
summary: Starfish Space has secured over $100 million in a Series B funding round led by Point72 Ventures, with co-leads Activate Capital and Shield Capital.

title: The State of EO 2026
summary: The Earth Observation (EO) industry is experiencing growth as more entities seek independent satellite capabilities. The 'State of EO 2026' report highlights this trend and its industry-wide benefits.

title: How Earthlings Got Views of the Lunar Fly By
summary: Redwire provided 11 cameras for the Orion capsule to capture views during its lunar flyby. These cameras were installed both inside and outside the spacecraft.

title: The State of Satcom 2026
summary: SpaceX and Amazon are disrupting the satcom market by 2026, forcing traditional players and startups to pivot and find innovative ways to compete.

title: Artemis II Supplier Series: Orion’s Windows
summary: The article discusses the importance of Orion's windows for astronauts on the Artemis II mission, highlighting the significance of seeing space firsthand after years of training. It is part of a series focusing on suppliers for the Artemis II mission.

title: White House Requests $18.8B FY27 Budget for NASA
summary: The White House has requested an $18.8 billion budget for NASA in fiscal year 2027, matching the previous year's request and maintaining similar cuts to science and STEM missions.

title: Insurance is Commercial Space Nuclear’s Biggest Headache
summary: Insurance is identified as the biggest challenge for commercial space nuclear missions, with experts considering it a 'show stopper'. The issue was highlighted during an industry seminar.

title: Charts Defining the Space Industry in Q1 2026
summary: The article discusses key trends and statistics defining the space industry in Q1 2026, with a focus on the resurgence of Earth Observation (EO) applications. It highlights the growing importance of EO in the commercial space sector.

title: CLPS Companies Excited For NASA’s ‘Opportunity Bomb’ Lunar Plan
summary: NASA announced a plan for a lunar base supplied through monthly uncrewed landings starting as soon as next year. CLPS companies are highly enthusiastic about this opportunity.

title: It’s Unanimous: Space Already Functions as Critical Infrastructure
summary: Experts agree that space functions as critical infrastructure in the U.S., despite not being officially designated as the 17th sector. The industry's deep integration into national systems highlights its importance.

title: Cybersecurity in Space is Hard; In Cislunar Space, it’s Really Hard
summary: Cybersecurity in space is challenging due to the lack of centralized anomaly detection and regular software patching. The difficulties are exacerbated in cislunar space, where traditional terrestrial cybersecurity strategies are less effective.

title: In-Space Mobility, Maturing From Last Miles to Superhighways
summary: NASA explored the concept of a reusable 'space tug' system in the late 1960s as part of the Space Transportation System. The idea is now evolving into a more advanced in-space mobility infrastructure.

title: Is Europe Moving Fast Enough to Close its Space Defense Gaps?
summary: Europe is accelerating efforts to address its space defense gaps amid concerns over Russia's military buildup and potential threats to NATO within the next four years. The urgency to strengthen space capabilities is highlighted as a critical response to evolving security challenges.

title: Space Is the Best Offset Strategy
summary: The article discusses space as a key national security offset strategy, leveraging technological advantages to counter adversarial strengths. It highlights the strategic importance of space in modern defense and deterrence.

title: The Seismic Shifts in the Satellite Communications Landscape are Far From Over
summary: The satellite communications market has seen rapid evolution over the past four years, driven significantly by SpaceX's Starlink constellation, which has deployed thousands of Low Earth Orbit (LEO) satellites.

title: Automation and Agility: How SSC Space Go Is Designed for the New Age of Ground
summary: SSC Space Go is designed to enhance automation and agility in accessing space through spacecraft operations, engineering, and ground network management. The company operates the Esrange launch center in Sweden as part of its mission.

title: On-Orbit Services Startup Starfish Space Raises $110M to Execute Missions, Boost Production
summary: Starfish Space raised $110 million in a Series B funding round to execute orbital servicing missions and boost production. The funds will support contracted missions and scale manufacturing capabilities.

title: Canadian Navy to Trial All.Space Hydra 4 Terminal
summary: The Royal Canadian Navy will test All.Space's Hydra 4 multi-band, multi-orbit mobility terminal to evaluate its satcom capabilities for future architecture decisions. The Hydra 4 terminal is designed to provide advanced satellite communication services.

title: Planet Details AI-Driven Object Detection Onboard Pelican-4 Satellite
summary: Planet Labs achieved a milestone by performing AI-driven object detection directly onboard a Pelican-4 satellite using an Nvidia Jetson Orin module. The company announced this achievement on Tuesday.
